display.purpose: Philosopher names at hero scale. Appears once per view, never in lists.

headline.purpose: Section headings within content areas. The scholarly voice of the product.

title.purpose: Era labels, period markers, institutional names. Always small and uppercase.

body.purpose: All interface text and prose. 65ch max line-length on content passages.

label.purpose: Metadata, dates, relationship tags, counts. Small and precise.

shadows.name: ambient-lift
shadows.purpose: Cards at rest. Reads as material presence, not decoration.

shadows.name: panel-rise
shadows.purpose: Open side panels, hovered cards, focused containers.

motion.name: ease-expo
motion.purpose: Primary easing. Entrances, panel slides, card hovers.

motion.name: ease-smooth
motion.purpose: Subtle state transitions: color, opacity, background shifts.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main call-to-action. Old Gold background, used sparingly — its rarity matches the accent rule.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #845400; color: #FCFBF9; padding: 10px 24px; border: none; border-radius: 4px; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.02em; cursor: pointer; transition: background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #C47029; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #845400; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action. Transparent at rest, Parchment Tint on hover. Used for supplementary actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: #11151A; padding: 8px 16px; border: 1px solid rgba(17,21,26,0.15); border-radius: 4px; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease, color 150ms ease, border-color 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: #F5EEE3; color: #845400; border-color: rgba(132,84,0,0.2); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #845400; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Era Chip
components.description: Era and philosophical branch labels. Cinzel uppercase, pill shape. Never used for actions.
components.css: .ds-era-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background: #F5EEE3; color: #845400; padding: 3px 10px; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: Cinzel, Georgia, serif; font-size: 0.65rem; font-weight: 400; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Philosopher Card
components.description: Content card for philosopher entries. Canvas Warm background, lifts on hover. Name in EB Garamond, metadata in Inter.
components.html:
Classical Antiquity

Aristotle

384–322 BCE

Pupil of Plato and founder of the Peripatetic school, systematizing logic, science, and ethics.
components.css: .ds-philosopher-card { background: #F8F5EE; border: 1px solid rgba(17,21,26,0.08); border-radius: 8px; padding: 20px 24px; box-shadow: 0 2px 12px rgba(17,21,26,0.06); transition: box-shadow 200ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), transform 200ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); cursor: pointer; } .ds-philosopher-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 4px 24px rgba(17,21,26,0.10), 0 1px 4px rgba(17,21,26,0.06); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-philosopher-card__era { margin-bottom: 10px; } .ds-philosopher-card__chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background: #F5EEE3; color: #845400; padding: 3px 10px; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: Cinzel, Georgia, serif; font-size: 0.6rem; font-weight: 400; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-philosopher-card__name { font-family: 'EB Garamond', Georgia, serif; font-size: 1.3rem; font-weight: 500; color: #11151A; line-height: 1.2; margin: 0 0 4px; } .ds-philosopher-card__dates { font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.6875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #5F6A78; letter-spacing: 0.06em; margin: 0 0 12px; } .ds-philosopher-card__bio { font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #5F6A78; line-height: 1.6; margin: 0; }

components.name: Nav Item
components.description: Sidebar navigation icon. Ash Gray at rest, Old Gold active. 80px sidebar, icon-only.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 40px; height: 40px; border-radius: 4px; color: #5F6A78; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease, color 150ms ease; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: #F5EEE3; color: #845400; } .ds-nav-item--active { background: #F5EEE3; color: #845400; } .ds-nav-item__icon { width: 20px; height: 20px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard text input. Flat at rest, Old Gold focus ring. Aged Vellum background.
components.css: .ds-input-wrapper { position: relative; } .ds-input { width: 100%; background: #FCFBF9; border: 1px solid rgba(17,21,26,0.15); border-radius: 6px; padding: 9px 14px; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.9375rem; color: #11151A; outline: none; transition: border-color 150ms ease, box-shadow 150ms ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #5F6A78; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #845400; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(132,84,0,0.12); }

narrative.overview:
A private, well-used intellectual space. The Living Manuscript treats philosophy not as a subject studied at a distance, but as a living conversation the user steps into. Every surface should feel inhabited: warm, slightly worn, full of accumulated meaning. Not pristine. Not sterile.

The system refuses three failure modes by name. First, the clinical neutrality of Wikipedia: text-dense, colorless, a filing cabinet that has forgotten it once held ideas. Second, the impersonal utility of SaaS dashboards: identical card grids, icon plus heading plus text repeated to exhaustion, utility without presence. Third, the cheap drama of dark philosophy aesthetics: brooding darkness, neon accents, glassmorphism as personality. This project earns atmosphere through restraint, not through effect.

The experience rewards wandering. A user arrives to look up one thinker and discovers three others through the network. The design should make that serendipity feel inevitable, not accidental.
keyCharacteristics: Parchment-warm surfaces; no pure whites, no pure blacks
Gold used sparingly as a signal of significance, never decoration
Serif typography carries content; sans-serif handles interface chrome only
Almost flat; depth through tonal layering, not dramatic shadow stacks
Era-specific color as contextual data embedded in the system, not cosmetic variety

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Old Gold appears on 10% or less of any given screen. It marks what is interactive and what is significant. Its scarcity is its authority. Warm Bronze signals state change only; it is never a second accent used in parallel with Old Gold at rest.

rules.name: The Register Rule
rules.body: If the user is reading content, the font is serif. If the user is navigating or scanning interface chrome, the font is Inter. A navigation item in Garamond is wrong. A philosopher biography in Inter is wrong. No exceptions.

rules.name: The Flat-by-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Shadows appear only in response to state: hover, open, focus. A shadow that is always visible is decoration. Decoration is prohibited.

dos: Do use Old Gold exclusively for interactive affordances and era markers. One accent, one role; its scarcity is the point.
Do use serif fonts (EB Garamond, Playfair Display) for all content the user reads; use Inter only for navigation and interface metadata.
Do layer depth through tonal backgrounds (Canvas Warm over Aged Vellum) before reaching for a shadow.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion by disabling all Framer Motion transitions when the media query matches.
Do use Cinzel only for era labels and period markers: small, uppercase, wide-tracked.
Do use full borders on cards and containers. All four sides, never just one.
donts: Don't make it feel like Wikipedia: clinical neutrality, text-dense, no visual warmth, no sense of place.
Don't make it feel like a generic SaaS dashboard: identical card grids with icon plus heading plus text, repeated without variation.
Don't use a gamified learning aesthetic: progress bars, achievement badges, Duolingo-style playfulness that trivializes the subject matter.
Don't use dark philosophy aesthetics: brooding dark mode, neon accents, dramatic purple gradients, glassmorphism as personality.
Don't use a side-stripe border (border-left or border-right greater than 1px in an accent color) on any card, callout, or list item.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text with a gradient). Single solid color only; emphasis through weight or scale.
Don't render philosopher cards as identical grid items. Scale and density should reflect variation in each thinker's significance and era.
Don't reach for a modal as the first response to any interaction. The Philosopher Panel slides in contextually; it does not interrupt the current view.
